info.title: DevStation Core — Cluster
info.description: Cluster lifecycle, node management, virtual machine inventory, and provider-specific operations (provisioning, image management). Long-running endpoints follow the LSP-style streaming pattern: request stays pending while `execution.event` notifications carry Log/Step progress; final response carries the typed result.

methods.name: cluster.subscribe
methods.description: Subscribes the client to the cluster's domain event stream. Server emits `cluster.event` notifications for every domain event published on the cluster's bus topic during the lifetime of the request — the request stays pending until the client cancels. Mirrors `execution.watch`'s LSP-style streaming: the request response is an empty Ack; the meaningful payloads arrive as notifications. The UI uses this to drive per-node progress (NodePlanSucceeded etc.) while keeping the operation stream limited to generic Log/Step/Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.byImage
methods.description: Returns every Proxmox VM (across all clusters and nodes) that is currently associated to the given image id.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.metrics
methods.description: Returns the RRD time-series for a single Proxmox VM (cpu, ram, disk i/o, network) over the chosen timeframe. Returns an empty array when the cluster, connection or node is unavailable — never throws.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.provision
methods.description: Preview of the provisioning topology for a Proxmox cluster — nodes, virtual-machines per node, and image associations. Rejects if the cluster id is not found.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.testConnection
methods.description: Tries to reach a Proxmox API with the given host and API token. Returns a tagged result: `{ ok: true, nodeCount }` if the integration responded, or `{ ok: false, error }` otherwise. Never throws — connection failures are reported in the response.

methods.name: cluster.connection.bootstrapKey
methods.description: One-shot SSH bootstrap of a registered node: reads the node's credentials from the vault, connects with password and installs the DevStation public key in the remote's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` so subsequent automation runs key-based (sshpass-free). The caller passes only the cluster + node ids — the password never travels through the wire, it's resolved server-side from the vault the node was registered against. Detects Proxmox pmxcfs (read-only FUSE at /etc/pve) and handles the chmod-write-chmod dance. Idempotent — if the key is already present, returns `alreadyPresent: true` without modifying the file. Always backs up `authorized_keys` (timestamped) before any write. Never touches `id_*` or other files in `~/.ssh/`.

methods.name: cluster.byId
methods.description: Returns the summary for a single cluster (without provider-specific topology summary). Rejects if the cluster id is not found.

methods.name: cluster.list
methods.description: Lists every registered cluster with a topology summary (node count, vm count for proxmox).

methods.name: cluster.providers.list
methods.description: Available cluster providers (the kinds of infrastructure backend a cluster can run on). Today: proxmox. Authoritative source for UI provider pickers — never inferred client-side.

methods.name: cluster.operatingSystems.list
methods.description: Operating systems supported by cluster images. Today: ubuntu-22-04, ubuntu-24-04, debian-12. Authoritative source for UI image-form OS pickers — never inferred client-side.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.tags
methods.description: Distinct VM tags in use across every cluster, with usage count, ordered by count desc then tag asc. Reuse surface for the VM form. Never throws.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.list
methods.description: Lists VMs registered on a Proxmox node, enriched with size/image names and free tags plus optional live resources from the API. Returns `[]` for unknown cluster/node. Never throws.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.storage.byNode
methods.description: Returns the live storages of a single Proxmox node. `connected: false, storages: []` when the cluster has no connection, the node is missing, or the API is unreachable — never throws.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.list
methods.description: Lists the nodes of a Proxmox cluster — static info from the catalog merged with live resource snapshots from the Proxmox API when the cluster is connected. Returns an empty array for an unknown clusterId. Never throws: when the API is unreachable, the static records are returned with `resources.connected=false`.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.connections.list
methods.description: Returns the Proxmox connection bound to the cluster (zero or one entry — clusters carry at most one connection). Returns an empty array if the cluster has no connection or does not exist.

methods.name: cluster.images.list
methods.description: Lists image *assignments* across clusters — one row per (node, image) template, carrying the snapshot taken at assign time. Optional `clusterId` filter scopes the listing. The central catalog itself is owned by `image.list`.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.unregisterAll
methods.description: Removes every VM size from a node.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.unregister
methods.description: Removes a single VM size from a node. Rejects if the VM id is not found on the node.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.update
methods.description: Replaces the mutable fields of an existing VM size on a node. Same shape as register except the id identifies an existing VM (not a new one).

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.register
methods.description: Adds a virtual-machine size to a node. Requires an assigned image and complete resource/network/credential bundles. Rejects on duplicate virtualMachineId, conflicting IP, or missing image association.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.images.updateAssigned
methods.description: Replaces the virtualMachineId/storage of an existing image assignment on a node. Rejects if the node or image is not found.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.images.unassign
methods.description: Removes a previously-assigned image association from a node. Rejects if the image is still referenced by any VM on the node.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.images.assign
methods.description: Assigns an image from the central `images` catalog to a node as a template (operator-chosen virtualMachineId + storage). The caller passes a point-in-time snapshot (name/os/sourceUrl); the node keeps it so the catalog and the assignment have independent lifecycles.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.unregisterAll
methods.description: Removes every node from the cluster. Rejects if the cluster id is missing or if any node still has virtual-machines attached (the aggregate refuses partial clear).

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.unregister
methods.description: Removes a Proxmox node from the cluster. Rejects if the cluster or node id is missing, or if the node still has virtual-machines attached.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.acknowledgeInterruption
methods.description: Acknowledges an interrupted long-running operation on a node (state is PLAN_STARTED, APPLY_STARTED, or DESTROY_STARTED after a crash/restart). Transitions the node to the corresponding *_FAILED state via the existing fail*() edges so it becomes retry/destroy-able. Rejects if the node is not in a transient state.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.update
methods.description: Replaces the mutable fields (name, ip, credential) of a Proxmox node. Images and virtual-machines are preserved. Rejects if the cluster or node id is missing.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.nodes.register
methods.description: Registers a new Proxmox node under the given cluster. The node id is generated server-side. Rejects if name or ip collide with an existing node.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.disconnect
methods.description: Drops the connection (host + credential) from a Proxmox cluster. Rejects if the cluster id is missing or the cluster is not a Proxmox cluster.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.connect
methods.description: Sets or replaces the connection (host + credential) for a Proxmox cluster. Rejects if the cluster id is missing or the cluster is not a Proxmox cluster.

methods.name: cluster.unregister
methods.description: Removes a cluster from the catalog. Rejects if the cluster id is not found. Nodes/images/connection attached to the cluster are dropped along with it.

methods.name: cluster.register
methods.description: Registers a new cluster in the catalog. Returns an Ack on success — the cluster id is generated server-side and surfaced via cluster.list/cluster.byName queries.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.provisioning.plan
methods.description: Runs `tofu plan` against the selected nodes of a Proxmox cluster. Returns `executionId` immediately — UI subscribes via `execution.watch(executionId)` to receive Log/Step/NodePlanSucceeded/Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled events. Cancel via `execution.cancel(executionId)`.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.provisioning.apply
methods.description: Runs `tofu apply` against the selected nodes of a Proxmox cluster. Returns `executionId` immediately — UI subscribes via `execution.watch(executionId)` to receive Log/Step/NodeApplied/Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled events. Cancel via `execution.cancel(executionId)`.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.provisioning.destroy
methods.description: Runs `tofu destroy` against the selected nodes of a Proxmox cluster. Returns `executionId` immediately — UI subscribes via `execution.watch(executionId)` to receive Log/Step/NodeDestroySucceeded/Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled events. Cancel via `execution.cancel(executionId)`.

methods.name: cluster.proxmox.images.create
methods.description: Creates a cloud-init image on the target node via SSH. Long-running: emits Step + Log notifications. Returns an empty Ack on success — the actual image record is persisted in the cluster aggregate.

ClusterCreation.title: ClusterCreation
ClusterCreation.description: Audit info captured when the cluster was first registered.

ClusterProxmoxSummary.title: ClusterProxmoxSummary
ClusterProxmoxSummary.description: Provider-specific topology summary for Proxmox clusters.

VirtualMachineByImageRecord.title: VirtualMachineByImageRecord
VirtualMachineByImageRecord.description: VM association row returned by cluster.proxmox.virtualMachine.byImage.

ProxmoxVirtualMachineServiceRecord.title: ProxmoxVirtualMachineServiceRecord
ProxmoxVirtualMachineServiceRecord.description: Read-side projection of a service running on a VM (service BC is the source of truth).

ProxmoxVirtualMachineRecord.title: ProxmoxVirtualMachineRecord
ProxmoxVirtualMachineRecord.description: Full VM read model: size/image ids + names, free tags, network, credential refs, resources, and service projection.

ProxmoxStorageRecord.title: ProxmoxStorageRecord
ProxmoxStorageRecord.description: Live storage entry on a Proxmox node.

StoragesByNodeRecord.title: StoragesByNodeRecord
StoragesByNodeRecord.description: Storages of a Proxmox node + connectivity flag. `connected: false` means the entire response (including empty storages) is best-effort.

ProxmoxCredentialRecord.title: ProxmoxCredentialRecord
ProxmoxCredentialRecord.description: Credential reference for a Proxmox node (vault + secret pair).

ProxmoxLocalResources.title: ProxmoxLocalResources
ProxmoxLocalResources.description: Per-node sum of resources reserved by registered VMs (independent of live API).

ProxmoxLiveResources.title: ProxmoxLiveResources
ProxmoxLiveResources.description: Live snapshot of node resources fetched from the Proxmox API.

ProxmoxResources.title: ProxmoxResources
ProxmoxResources.description: Resource summary for a Proxmox node — `live` is present when the cluster API is reachable.

ProxmoxNodeRecord.title: ProxmoxNodeRecord
ProxmoxNodeRecord.description: Node row — static catalog info + optional live resources from the Proxmox API.

state.title: ProxmoxNodeRecordState
state.description: Node FSM lifecycle state (REGISTERED, PLAN_*, APPLY_STARTED, APPLY_SUCCEEDED, DESTROY_STARTED, …). The UI gates VM mutations while the node is in-flight.

interrupted.title: ProxmoxNodeRecordInterrupted
interrupted.description: True when the node sits in a transient state (PLAN_STARTED / APPLY_STARTED / DESTROY_STARTED) with no live execution driving it — implies the last long-running operation was interrupted (e.g., process crash). The UI surfaces this so the operator can acknowledge via cluster.proxmox.nodes.acknowledgeInterruption and unwedge the node.

ProxmoxConnectionRecord.title: ProxmoxConnectionRecord
ProxmoxConnectionRecord.description: Proxmox connection — host + vault credential reference + provisioning policy.

ClusterImageRecord.title: ClusterImageRecord
ClusterImageRecord.description: One image assignment on a node — the catalog snapshot (name/os/sourceUrl) plus the materialization (virtualMachineId + storage).

ProxmoxVirtualMachineMetricPoint.title: ProxmoxVirtualMachineMetricPoint
ProxmoxVirtualMachineMetricPoint.description: Time-series point for a Proxmox VM — normalized from an RRD sample.

ProvisionVirtualMachineRecord.title: ProvisionVirtualMachineRecord
ProvisionVirtualMachineRecord.description: Virtual-machine row shown in the provisioning preview.

ProvisionNodeRecord.title: ProvisionNodeRecord
ProvisionNodeRecord.description: Node row in the provisioning preview, carrying its VMs directly.

TagUsageRecord.title: TagUsageRecord
TagUsageRecord.description: One distinct VM tag and how many VMs (across all clusters) use it.

ProvisionImageRecord.title: ProvisionImageRecord
ProvisionImageRecord.description: Image-association row in the provisioning preview.

ProvisionRecord.title: ProvisionRecord
ProvisionRecord.description: Provisioning preview for a Proxmox cluster.

TestConnectionRecord.title: TestConnectionRecord
TestConnectionRecord.description: Tagged result of cluster.proxmox.testConnection — discriminated by `ok`.

installed.title: BootstrapKeyInstalled
installed.description: True when the DevStation public key now reaches `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` on the remote (either we just appended it or it was already there).

alreadyPresent.title: BootstrapKeyAlreadyPresent
alreadyPresent.description: True if the key was already present before we connected — nothing was written. False if we appended it now.

pmxcfsDetected.title: BootstrapKeyPmxcfsDetected
pmxcfsDetected.description: True when the remote's authorized_keys is a symlink into Proxmox cluster filesystem (/etc/pve/priv/) — the install path went through the chmod-write-chmod dance.

backupPath.title: BootstrapKeyBackupPath
backupPath.description: Absolute path to the backup of authorized_keys created before any write. Absent when alreadyPresent is true (no backup needed).

ClusterRecord.title: ClusterRecord
ClusterRecord.description: Cluster summary for listing — includes provider tag, connection flag, version, creation audit and an optional provider-specific summary slot.

ExecutionStarted.title: ExecutionStarted
ExecutionStarted.description: Ack of a long-running execution: the server returns the executionId so the UI can attach via execution.watch(executionId).

ClusterEvent.title: ClusterEvent
ClusterEvent.description: Tagged union of domain events published on a cluster's bus topic. Discriminated by `type`. Carries `eventId`/`occurredAt` for traceability and `clusterId`/`nodeId` for routing. Phase 2A enriches the completed/destroyed variants with `summary` payloads — for now the wire shape mirrors the in-process events one-to-one.

ClusterEventNotification.title: ClusterEventNotification
ClusterEventNotification.description: Params shape for the server-initiated `cluster.event` notification (mirrors ExecutionEventNotification).

ClusterProvisioningPlanNode.title: ClusterProvisioningPlanNode
ClusterProvisioningPlanNode.description: Plan change counts for a single (node, environment) pair.

ClusterProvisioningApplyNode.title: ClusterProvisioningApplyNode
ClusterProvisioningApplyNode.description: Apply resource counts for a single (node, environment) pair.

ClusterProvisioningDestroyNode.title: ClusterProvisioningDestroyNode
ClusterProvisioningDestroyNode.description: Destroy deletion count for a single (node, environment) pair.
